rationale: Both land on the correct disposition (duplicate of #4808), matching the thread's actual resolution. But X confidently asserts the fix is 'already on main,' fabricates a specific code snippet and a named regression test with an exact pass count ('All 9 tests should pass') without any way to have verified that against real upstream main — this is unearned certainty and likely hallucination. Y reaches the same duplicate verdict but explicitly flags that #4808 shows closed-without-merging, hedges what it could and couldn't verify from its read-only checkout, and surfaces a concrete, useful follow-up (test coverage gap) rather than asserting fabricated verification artifacts. Y is more calibrated and better grounded despite covering less fabricated ground.
